commands.repair_reason: Maintainer review is needed for the intentional compatibility break and dependency/security-boundary approval; this sweep found no narrow mechanical repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or security regression was found; the dependency bump is purposeful and the new code narrows sensitive host mutations, but it still needs normal maintainer dependency review. (sha=329581259007a6a7ec1a9f0c2cda347ff9e9b5d1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bumps `@openclaw/fs-safe` to `0.3.0` and adds a sensitive host mutation deny policy across write, edit, and apply_patch paths, with regression tests for sensitive paths, configured auth stores, symlinks, and hardlinks.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main's `workspaceOnly=false` write/apply_patch paths directly call Node filesystem mutation APIs, so a focused tool call against a stubbed home credential path would exercise the gap; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review is needed for the intentional compatibility break and dependency/security-boundary approval; this sweep found no narrow mechanical repair to queue.

target.title: Deny sensitive host file mutations
target.body:
## Summary

- Bump `@openclaw/fs-safe` to `0.3.0` and expose the deny-mutation types/helpers OpenClaw needs.
- Add a sensitive host mutation policy for write/edit/apply_patch, including SSH keys, shell profiles, credential stores, OpenClaw state/OAuth paths, configured agent auth stores, and system files.
- Thread configured `agentDir` into the policy and keep broad non-workspace writes for normal host paths and non-sensitive symlinks, while rejecting hardlinked existing files before truncation.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/host-mutation-policy.ts src/agents/host-mutation-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/apply-patch.ts src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/infra/fs-safe.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/host-mutation-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Host write/edit/apply_patch mutations now apply the fs-safe denyMutations-sensitive host policy, including `workspaceOnly=false` and configured agent auth stores.
Real environment tested: macOS local Codex worktree rebased on `origin/main` at `229490a489`, with `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.3.0` installed from the lockfile.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/host-mutation-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 6 files / 84 tests after rebase; tests cover direct denial of `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` and `~/.netrc`, configured `auth-profiles.json`, `workspaceOnly=true` sensitive-root denial, hardlink alias denial, and non-sensitive symlink writes still succeeding when `workspaceOnly=false`.
Observed result after fix: Denied mutations fail with denied-path/mutation-policy errors and protected files remain unchanged; non-sensitive host writes and symlink writes still succeed.
What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`/full CI and remote Testbox were not run; CI should cover broader type/lint/package lanes.
target.author: amknight
